content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Confirmed the bootstrap review gate was already wired in the CLI and requirements service, then added regression coverage for the default skip path and the explicit `--enable-review` path. Also hardened `WorktreeAdapter` git binary discovery and applied rustfmt to clear unrelated validation blockers so repository-wide checks could pass.

## Steps

1. [Completed] Inspect the bead and bootstrap requirements flow; confirmed `project bootstrap` already threads `--enable-review` into `RequirementsService::quick` and gates the review loop in quick mode.
2. [Completed] Extend CLI regression coverage so default bootstrap asserts no requirements review is persisted or journaled, and `--enable-review` asserts a review payload and `review_completed` journal entry are produced.
3. [Completed] Fix `WorktreeAdapter` git executable discovery and format the existing daemon loop file so repository-wide validation can run cleanly in this environment.
4. [Completed] Run targeted and repository-wide validation, then close bead `ralph-burning-kvc` in `br`.

## Validation Evidence

- `cargo test --features test-stub --test cli project_bootstrap_` passed.
- `cargo test` passed.
- `cargo clippy` completed successfully; only existing warnings remain.
- `cargo fmt --check` passed after formatting `src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs`.
- `br close ralph-burning-kvc --reason "Completed"` succeeded.
- `br sync --flush-only` reported no dirty issues to export.

## Outstanding Risks

- `br lint` still reports pre-existing template warnings on unrelated beads (`ralph-burning-rlm.*`, `ralph-burning-utm`, `ralph-burning-gsp`, `ralph-burning-p2h`, `ralph-burning-fyj`, `ralph-burning-hg4`).
